creatives.headline: 375 Studies. One Takeaway.
creatives.caption:
JAMA Psychiatry just published the largest CBT meta-analysis in history — 375 randomized controlled trials, 32,968 patients.

The key finding: CBT effect sizes vary by 4x depending on the condition. PTSD? Highly responsive (1.27). Bipolar? Much less so (0.31).

The takeaway isn't that CBT doesn't work. It's that the right match changes everything.

Online therapy with 80+ specialists across multiple modalities.
manhattanmhc.com

creatives.headline: The 4x Gap
creatives.caption:
When we say "therapy works," we need to ask: for what?

The latest JAMA data shows a 4x difference in CBT effectiveness:
• PTSD: 1.27 (strong)
• Anxiety: 0.5-1.0 (moderate-strong)
• Depression: 0.5-1.0 (moderate-strong)
• Bipolar: 0.31 (limited)

This is why we don't do one-size-fits-all. 80+ therapists, multiple modalities.
manhattanmhc.com

creatives.headline: Why We Offer More Than CBT
creatives.caption:
CBT gets the headlines, but it's not the only evidence-based approach.

• DBT: 68% reduction in suicide attempts for bipolar (JAMA 2024)
• IPSRT: stabilizes circadian rhythms disrupted by bipolar
• FFT: prevents mood episodes in high-risk youth

We hire therapists across all of these modalities — because one approach can't cover every condition.

80+ licensed therapists online.
manhattanmhc.com
creatives.research_cite: Goldstein et al. 2024; Steardo et al. 2020

creatives.headline: 1 in 4 Quit Trauma Therapy
creatives.caption:
Here's the paradox: PTSD responds most strongly to therapy (effect size 1.27) but has a 24% dropout rate — the highest of any condition studied.

Dropout isn't a character flaw. It's a clinical challenge. The right therapist-client fit, the right pacing, the right modality — these keep people in treatment.

We take retention as seriously as treatment.
manhattanmhc.com

creatives.headline: When CBT Isn't Enough
creatives.caption:
CBT addresses thought patterns. But bipolar disorder involves circadian disruption, emotional dysregulation, and family dynamics that CBT wasn't designed for.

The research:
• DBT: 68% fewer suicide attempts (Goldstein 2024)
• FFT: prevents episodes in high-risk youth (Miklowitz 2020)
• IPSRT: stabilizes sleep-wake cycles (Steardo 2020)

The right therapy depends on what you're treating.
manhattanmhc.com
creatives.research_cite: Goldstein et al. 2024; Miklowitz et al. 2020; Steardo et al. 2020

creatives.headline: The Control Group Problem
creatives.caption:
"Evidence-based" is a starting point, not a finish line.

When you read that therapy "works," ask:
• Compared to what? (Waitlist? Medication? Another therapy?)
• For whom? (Age, condition, severity?)
• How much? (Effect size tells you magnitude, not just direction)

We hire therapists who think about research this carefully. Because your care deserves nuance.
manhattanmhc.com

creatives.headline: What to Ask Your Therapist
creatives.caption:
You're allowed to ask your therapist hard questions:

1. What's the evidence for this approach with my condition?
2. Have you treated this before? How often?
3. What's your plan if this approach isn't working after 8 weeks?
4. Are there other modalities I should consider?

A good therapist welcomes these questions. They're not challenges — they're collaboration.
manhattanmhc.com

creatives.headline: Matching Matters More Than Method
creatives.caption:
Why do we have 80+ therapists?

Not because bigger is better. Because matching is everything.

The latest JAMA research confirms: therapy effectiveness varies 4x by condition. That means the right therapist-condition match determines your outcome more than any single method.

80+ therapists. CBT, DBT, EMDR, IPSRT, and more. Online across New York State.
manhattanmhc.com

creatives.headline: Bipolar Deserves Better Than Generic CBT
creatives.caption:
Bipolar disorder is one of the hardest conditions to treat with standard CBT (effect size just 0.31).

But that doesn't mean therapy doesn't work for bipolar. It means different therapy works:
• DBT: 68% fewer suicide attempts
• FFT: prevents mood episodes
• IPSRT: stabilizes daily rhythms

We offer all of these. Because bipolar deserves specialized care.
manhattanmhc.com
creatives.research_cite: Goldstein et al. 2024; Cuijpers et al. 2025

creatives.headline: Online Therapy. Same Results, Different Setting.
creatives.caption:

What you gain:

80+ therapists. Fully online. Insurance accepted.
